items.content: Raíz: Carlos Mendoza's modern Mexican restaurant in Mexico City. He is an executive chef and co-owner here, focusing on contemporary interpretations of traditional Mexican cuisine. It's the central hub of his professional culinary identity.
Culinary Institute of Mexico: The institution where Carlos Mendoza received his formal culinary training. This foundational education shaped his professional skills and approach to cooking.
Pre-Hispanic Ingredients: Carlos has a specialisation in reinventing these ancient ingredients for modern tasting menus at Raíz. This focus distinguishes his restaurant and culinary style.
Youth Football Team: Carlos coaches a weekend youth football team. This indicates a commitment to his community and potentially a passion for sports outside of his culinary career.
Vintage Mezcal Collection: Carlos collects vintage mezcal. This hobby suggests an interest in artisanal spirits, Mexican heritage, and potentially a connoisseur's palate.
Rooftop Herb Garden: Carlos tends a rooftop herb garden, likely supplying fresh ingredients for Raíz. This personal connection to his produce highlights his dedication to farm-to-table principles.
Madrid: Carlos staged at restaurants in Madrid as part of his culinary training. This experience likely exposed him to different European culinary techniques and influences.
Paris: Similar to Madrid, Carlos also completed a stage in Paris. This further broadened his culinary horizons with exposure to renowned French gastronomy.
